customer_addr:
10811 S Deer Lake Road
Reed City, MI 49677

citations.narrative:
The facility is not keeping the premises and grounds clean in order to protect the animals from injury and to facilitate
prescribed husbandry practices.
• Hayloft: The hayloft containing five tarp-covered bales of hay has a thick layer of hay on most of the floor. Three
piles of racoon feces are in the corner of the room about 35 feet from the hay.
• Storage room: Old animal crates, a packed roll of insulation, and other old equipment were being stored
temporarily in the storage room inside the barn. There is an area of rodent feces behind items stored along the wall.
• Outside the barn: There is an area of debris outside the animal barn with tires, furniture, and other discarded
items. This area extends approximately 50 by 20 feet and is adjacent to animal enclosures. Couch cushion stuffing from
the area had escaped the cushion and was observed scattered in the adjacent enclosure, which was currently not in use.
Lack of housekeeping and sanitation can attract pests into the facility, resulting in unpleasant odors, and may contribute
to the spread of disease. Accumulations of debris can also provide areas which can harbor disease-transmitting pests and
could increase health risks to the animals. Correct by ensuring that premises (buildings and grounds) are kept clean, in
good repair and accumulations of trash are placed in designated areas and cleared as necessary in order to protect the
animals from injury, protect their health, and to facilitate prescribed husbandry practices.
Correct by: April 13, 2026
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class "C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by May 25, 2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
3.131(c) 
   
Sanitation. 
The facility is not keeping the premises and grounds clean in order to protect the animals from injury and to facilitate 
prescribed husbandry practices. 
•  Hayloft: The hayloft containing five tarp-covered bales of hay has a thick layer of hay on most of the floor. Three 
piles of racoon feces are in the corner of the room about 35 feet from the hay.  
•  Storage room: Old animal crates, a packed roll of insulation, and other old equipment were being stored 
temporarily in the storage room inside the barn. There is an area of rodent feces behind items stored along the wall.  
•  Outside the barn: There is an area of debris outside the animal barn with tires, furniture, and other discarded 
items. This area extends approximately 50 by 20 feet and is adjacent to animal enclosures. Couch cushion stuffing from 
the area had escaped the cushion and was observed scattered in the adjacent enclosure, which was currently not in use.  
 
Lack of housekeeping and sanitation can attract pests into the facility, resulting in unpleasant odors, and may contribute 
to the spread of disease. Accumulations of debris can also provide areas which can harbor disease-transmitting pests and 
could increase health risks to the animals. Correct by ensuring that premises (buildings and grounds) are kept clean, in 
good repair and accumulations of trash are placed in designated areas and cleared as necessary in order to protect the 
animals from injury, protect their health, and to facilitate prescribed husbandry practices. 
 
Correct by: April 13, 2026 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class "C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by May 25, 2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee 
and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.

n
